title: Marionette Lines: Which Dermal Filler Is Best？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Marionette Lines： Which Dermal Filler Is Best？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So which dermal filler is best for marionette lines every manufacturer makes a range of products and they tend to be either a very light one for superficial so fine lines increases a medium depth dermal filler and a deeper one that's a little bit more like bone and now there are …

segments.text: so which dermal filler is best for marionette lines every manufacturer makes a range of products and they tend to be either a very light one for superficial so fine lines increases a medium depth dermal filler and a deeper one that's a little bit more like bone and now there are a few product few companies producing on either side of that products that are much more like bone in the analogous case Institute and bollocks and on the other end of the spectrum you have manufacturers who make products for the surface of the skin that's like Juvederm though light or profile o is really well known in this area and these are super hydrating more superficial products that aren't true dermal fillers they're more about holding moisture in the surface of the skin but very similar so in terms of treating marionette lines now the first thing to say about this is often the best treatment is more holistic with these lines because they are the endpoint of all sorts of changes happening higher up in the face so I very rarely see them as the isolated problem now in someone with a mild

change in their middle a B or fold maybe it's appropriate to start with a little bit one mil that you might use just as the sport just to support the skin there in which cases I'd be using an intermediate depth filler I don't want to use a very hard product or a third product that's meant to emulate bone because people will feel it there it's a sensitive area can use it's a functional area so the sensation of something there that's too thick would be obvious for many people and I also don't want to use something too light because the problem is it just won't resist the degree of force on the weight is coming from the cheeks rotating forward so an intermediate fella tends to work best and but a really good-quality thicker product and some of them I actually do quite often use Juvederm voluma there because it is quite a soft product when you first put it in one even when it integrates it's also strong so I do use that sometimes but it wouldn't be my first choice unless it's a slightly heavier fold
